data.output:
ID:       ddx-5d49b14e
Title:    Evaluate and adopt a real backend for the bead tracker (axon primary, bd fallback)
Type:     epic
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, area:beads, area:storage, kind:strategy, backend-migration, triage:needs-investigation
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-b18d9ce6
Desc:     The current .ddx/beads.jsonl backend (atomic JSONL writes wrapped in flock; events accumulating per-bead; archive split per ADR-004) is showing strain:

1. Multi-worker contention via the git tracker-commit step (see ddx-da11a34a — the file lock around git add/commit is a v1 patch, not the real fix)
2. Events array per bead is unbounded and dominates archive size
3. Cross-machine concurrency is not addressed (Story 14 federation will require it)
4. The current model bakes one process-local store + git-as-replication; doesn't compose cleanly with a real database

Two backend paths under consideration:

**Axon (PRIMARY, per user inclination)**: integrate as the first DDx-side consumer of axon. User owns the stack; tight integration possible; proves axon's value as a substrate. Requires authoring an axon backend implementing the bead schema's storage contract (per ADR-004 / SD-004 — collection abstraction, claim semantics, atomic writes, JSONL interchange compatibility for bd/br).

**bd / doltdb (FALLBACK)**: delegate to bd which already speaks doltdb (git-for-data). Trivial integration; downside is tracking an external codebase as a hard dependency.

This epic frames the evaluation, designs the integration, prototypes, and migrates. Children break down the work. The lock-contention patch (ddx-da11a34a) remains relevant as stability scaffolding while this plays out — when axon ships and is adopted, the JSONL+git path may retire entirely (or stay as a portable interchange backend per FEAT-004's bd/br semantics).
Accept:   1. TD authored for the axon-backend integration (data model mapping, claim/lock semantics, archive policy, multi-machine concurrency story). 2. Axon-backend implementation lands behind a feature flag or selectable backend in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. 3. Migration tool moves an existing .ddx/beads.jsonl + .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl into the axon backend without data loss. 4. Existing chaos_test.go suite passes against the axon backend. 5. bd fallback documented (single artifact under docs/) showing how to wire bd as the backend if axon adoption stalls. 6. Performance: queue ops (ready/blocked/show) at parity or better than JSONL on the current 1100-bead archive scale.
Created:  2026-05-02 13:06:50
Updated:  2026-05-03 05:17:03
Claimed:  2026-05-03T05:16:03Z

input.content:
no_changes
status: needs_investigation
verification_command: bash -c 'set -e; for id in ddx-6bb51a52 ddx-bbdd7564 ddx-95ec5ed5 ddx-cb63cdfc ddx-743bc194 ddx-8835a765 ddx-6602559c; do ddx bead show "$id" >/dev/null || { echo "missing $id"; exit 1; }; done'

reason: Epic ddx-5d49b14e decisively fails the Step 0 size check — 6 ACs span unrelated
subsystems (TD authoring, backend interface refactor, new axon implementation, migration
tool, chaos-test integration, fallback documentation, performance benchmarking). Per the
execute-bead instructions, the correct action when a bead is too big is to decompose, not
attempt the work. A prior whole-epic attempt was BLOCKED in post-merge review (events log)
for producing only execution evidence and not addressing any AC.

The decomposition has already been performed in a prior attempt and the seven children
exist in the tracker with correct parent edges and dependency DAG (verified in this
attempt via `ddx bead show` and `ddx bead show <child>` for each):

  ddx-6bb51a52  TD: design axon-backend integration for bead tracker
  ddx-bbdd7564  Introduce selectable bead Backend interface in cli/internal/bead   (deps: ddx-6bb51a52)
  ddx-95ec5ed5  Implement axon Backend for bead tracker                            (deps: ddx-bbdd7564)
  ddx-cb63cdfc  Migration tool: .ddx/beads.jsonl + archive -> axon backend         (deps: ddx-95ec5ed5)
  ddx-743bc194  Run chaos_test.go against axon backend                             (deps: ddx-95ec5ed5)
  ddx-8835a765  Document bd/doltdb fallback backend wiring                         (deps: ddx-bbdd7564)
  ddx-6602559c  Bench: queue ops parity (axon vs JSONL) on 1100-bead archive      (deps: ddx-95ec5ed5)

This epic should remain open as the rollup; close it when all seven children land and
each passes post-merge review. Triage / orchestrator should now drain the children rather
than re-dispatching the epic. The lock-contention v1 patch (ddx-da11a34a) remains the
stability scaffolding while this plays out and is independent of this decomposition.

The verification_command above exits 0 iff all seven decomposition children still exist
in the tracker, which is the concrete, machine-checkable evidence that the decomposition
work is complete for this epic.

input.command: bash -c 'set -e; for id in ddx-6bb51a52 ddx-bbdd7564 ddx-95ec5ed5 ddx-cb63cdfc ddx-743bc194 ddx-8835a765 ddx-6602559c; do ddx bead show "$id" >/dev/null || { echo "missing $id"; exit 1; }; done' && echo OK

Stopping per the no_changes contract — bead is correctly decomposed into 7 child beads (already present in tracker with parent + dependency edges), and the rationale at `.ddx/executions/20260503T051603-07c1516a/no_changes_rationale.txt` carries both `status: needs_investigation` and a passing `verification_command`. No commit per the no_changes pattern.
